title: Chamaesyce tettensis
taxonomy: Chamaesyce tettensis is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family, Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Chamaesyce, which is a group of succulent plants found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species Chamaesyce tettensis is native to the tropical forests of Madagascar and is known for its small, yellowish-green flowers. Chamaesyce tettensis is often classified as a low-growing, spreading plant with small, rounded leaves and a short, woody stem. As a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, Chamaesyce tettensis is closely related to other spurge species found in similar habitats.

intro: Chamaesyce tettensis, also known as the Tette Spurge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its succulent stems and small, white flowers. The Tette Spurge has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Chamaesyce tettensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
